title: Vangueriella nigerica
intro: Vangueriella nigerica, also known as Bush Mango, is a medicinal plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and diarrhea. The bioactive constituents of Vangueriella nigerica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or decoction, and it is also used to make various herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments.
taxonomy: Vangueriella nigerica is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae. This family of flowering plants is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Vangueriella nigerica is further classified within the genus Vangueriella, which is a group of flowering plants endemic to tropical Africa. The classification of Vangueriella nigerica is also supported by its unique morphological characteristics, such as its distinctive leaf shape and flower arrangement. Overall, the classification of Vangueriella nigerica reflects its distinct position within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Vangueriella nigerica has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's extracts are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Vangueriella nigerica is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves are crushed and applied topically to treat wounds and skin irritations. However, further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.

parts: Vangueriella nigerica is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa. Its leaves, roots, and bark are the primary medicinal parts, utilized to treat various ailments. The leaves are often used to manage fever, while the roots are employed to treat stomach issues and other health conditions. Additionally, the bark is used to treat skin problems and wounds. preparations: Vangueriella nigerica is traditionally used in folk medicine in West Africa. Herbal preparations of Vangueriella nigerica are believed to have various health benefits, including treatment of fever, malaria, and other illnesses.
